evidence: Repository purpose and deployed surfaces identified: Expo app, Supabase backend/functions, Next website/admin.
Authenticated users can manage their own web_push_subscriptions rows with endpoint/p256dh/auth/timezone fields; no host/scheme or PushManager-origin proof is enforced in the database policy.
Normal client path obtains endpoint and keys from browser PushManager before upserting, but the same table is writable by authenticated users through Supabase Data API.
Cron-authenticated service-role function reads all subscription endpoints and passes each endpoint to webPush.sendNotification.
Independent cron-authenticated service-role function reads all subscription endpoints and passes each endpoint to webPush.sendNotification.
Unauthenticated completion endpoint requires HMAC action token verified by _shared/push-action-token.ts and scopes writes to user/habit/date.
Action tokens validate signature, UUID/date shapes, and expiry before service-role completion writes.
Admin layout redirects unless current Supabase user email is included in ADMIN_EMAILS.
Privileged server actions call requireAdmin before service-role mutations.
Privileged feature flag and notification actions call requireAdmin before service-role mutations.
Admin content actions call requireAdmin; inputs are inserted via Supabase client rather than query-string construction.
Feedback status mutation calls requireAdmin and validates status enum.
Callback next parameter is normalized with same-origin check before redirect.
Client next path rejects scheme URLs and protocol-relative paths.
Webhook requires shared auth token and re-fetches subscriber entitlement state from RevenueCat using secret API key before profile upsert.
Authenticated user identity from JWT selects RevenueCat subscriber id; no request body user id is trusted.
Account deletion verifies JWT user and recent sign-in before service-role deletion of that same user id.
Feedback email requires JWT, constrains category, and escapes user-controlled HTML fields before sending.
Leaderboard function requires JWT and uses service-role RPCs with fixed period/limit parsing and current user id from auth.
AI routine endpoint requires JWT, Pro access, AI quota, local recommendation schema validation, and bounded model output schema parsing.
AI coach endpoint requires JWT, Pro access, AI quota, and cleans message fields before invoking Gemini.
AI smart reminder endpoint requires JWT, Pro/quota checks, and structured context/output handling.
Habit validation endpoint requires JWT and sanitizes habit fields/enums before Gemini call; failure is fail-open for wellness UX, not a cross-user security boundary.
Generate-now path requires JWT and Pro access; cron batch requires x-cron-secret before service-role processing.
Public OG renderer uses query parameters in React/ImageResponse text nodes and fixed local font paths; no file path or HTML execution sink found.
Notification completion state change goes through signed completeToken; tap navigation uses server-supplied payload from controlled push sender paths.
Authenticated insert/update privileges on profiles are narrowed so entitlement columns are not user-writable.

Core habit/completion/sleep/profile/feedback/deletion RLS policies are owner-scoped; notable service-role and security-definer functions have explicit grants/revokes reviewed for high-impact bypasses.

path: app/, components/, lib/ client data flows
evidence: Client-side mutations generally rely on Supabase RLS and current user identity; no independently reachable high-impact sink beyond push subscription endpoint storage was promoted.

These paths appeared in the parent worklist but are generated, design, alternate worktree, output, promo, test, or documentation artifacts without repository evidence of direct deployed authority for this scan pass.
Consumed parent-provided authoritative rank_input.csv exactly as supplied; row count observed: 1010; not regenerated or reranked.
Consumed parent-provided authoritative deep_review_input.csv exactly as supplied; row count observed: 1010; not regenerated or overwritten.
Read project overview: Expo app, Supabase backend/functions, and separate Next admin/marketing site.
Server actions call requireAdmin; requireAdmin verifies Supabase current user and ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist before service-role actions.
Admin page tree redirects non-admin users based on the same ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist before rendering admin pages.
Grant/revoke/reset/verify/delete actions all call requireAdmin before createAdminClient; no unguarded service-role action observed.
Feature flag and notification mutations call requireAdmin before service-role updates/inserts.
Suggested habit create/update/toggle/delete actions call requireAdmin before service-role mutations.
Middleware validates Supabase user for protected app pages and bypasses /app PWA rewrites deliberately; no cross-origin redirect found in auth flow.
Callback next parameter is normalized with new URL and same-origin check before redirect.
Functions with verify_jwt=false identified: revenuecat-webhook, progress-report, welcome-email, complete-habit-from-push.
Unauthenticated function requires configured Authorization token and refetches subscriber state from RevenueCat before service-role profile upsert.
generate-now mode verifies user JWT; cron mode requires x-cron-secret before service-role batch generation.
verify_jwt=false function requires x-welcome-secret, is idempotent per profile, and sends fixed welcome content to supplied email.
verify_jwt=false function accepts only HMAC action token, validates shape/expiry, checks habit user ownership, then upserts completion.
HMAC token verifier checks signature, UUID/date shape, and exp before returning payload.
Service-role cron reads stored web_push_subscriptions and passes sub.endpoint directly to webPush.sendNotification without destination validation.
Service-role coach cron reads stored web_push_subscriptions and passes sub.endpoint directly to webPush.sendNotification; feature flag is a precondition, not endpoint validation.
web_push_subscriptions.endpoint is unconstrained text; owner_all RLS lets authenticated users manage own rows.
Normal client obtains endpoint from PushManager and upserts by endpoint; this is a normal-path source but not a server-side enforcement control against direct Data API writes.
Service worker fetches completeUrl only from signed push payload data for Mark done action; complete-habit function validates HMAC token server-side.
Edge function verifies Authorization with Supabase anon client before service-role RPC calls; period and limit are constrained.
Leaderboard RPC parameters are bounded; execute grants restricted to service_role.
Direct public/authenticated grants on leaderboard and public_profiles are revoked and service_role-only grants applied.
Column grants revoke authenticated profile entitlement writes; only safe profile columns remain writable by authenticated users.
Function verifies caller JWT, fetches RevenueCat by user.id, and upserts only caller profile subscription state.
Function verifies caller JWT and recent sign-in before service-role deletion of that user's data/auth user.
Function verifies Authorization token with Supabase auth; message/category/email values are escaped or constrained before Resend HTML.
Function verifies user JWT, enforces Pro and AI quota before Gemini call, and sanitizes returned recommendations before returning to client.
Function verifies user JWT, validates context shape/enums/times, enforces Pro and AI quota, and schema-constrains response.
Function verifies user JWT and enforces Pro/quota before Gemini call; output is cleaned to a short message.

Code includes validate-habit in AiFeature and fails open to okResult when quota guard unavailable; latest reviewed SQL whitelist did not include validate-habit. Tracked as safety-control mismatch, not promoted as security candidate.

The supplied 1010-row worklist includes generated caches (.next), historical worktrees, design prototypes, docs/tests, and runtime files. This worker performed full frontier review of deployed security boundaries and recorded non-runtime categories in coverage rather than claiming full line-by-line review of generated/historical rows.

notes: Generated independent repository-level threat model for worker-specific use only.
Consumed parent-provided worklists as read-only shared inputs; did not regenerate, rerank, overwrite, or reinterpret them.
Entitlement columns plus owner update policy create a plausible self-upgrade path in deployments that use the admin schema without the later column-level grant hardening.
Admin pages are nested under the admin layout and server actions call requireAdmin() before service-role use.
Redirect parameters are restricted to same-origin relative paths; OG text is rendered through React/Satori and styling choices are allowlisted or defaulted.
No-JWT functions reviewed add compensating controls: shared secrets, provider refetch, authenticated mode split, or HMAC action tokens.
Reviewed functions verify Authorization tokens or user identity before service-role reads/writes and scope privileged operations to the verified user or fixed server-side policy.
Cron-triggered jobs require per-function secrets; service worker fetch caching is same-origin; push completion needs a signed action token.
Server-only keys are referenced from server contexts; public config uses anon/client-safe values. Local env files were not opened or copied.
Emitted one distinct, technically plausible candidate with unified affected_locations shape.
No semantic duplicates found within this worker output.
Recorded reviewed families, dispositions, and remaining validation needs for later centralized phases.

evidence: Repository purpose, deployment surfaces, environment variables, and primary app/backend layout reviewed.
Root Expo app dependencies and scripts reviewed.
Next website/admin dependency surface reviewed.
No-JWT functions identified and traced to function-local auth/shared-secret controls.
Subscription endpoint storage and owner_all RLS reviewed; endpoint destination and cardinality controls are incomplete.
Service-role cron reads all subscriptions and sends to stored endpoints.
Second service-role push sender reads all subscriptions and sends to stored endpoints when feature/signal gates pass.
Normal browser path obtains PushManager subscription and upserts endpoint, but database policy also permits direct authenticated writes.
Push action token is scoped to user/habit/date and redeemed through HMAC-verified endpoint; no standalone candidate promoted.
HMAC action token is verified, habit ownership is checked, and write is idempotent for a specific day.
Token signature, UUID/date shape, and expiry checks reviewed.
Direct function boundary checks JWT but not feedback length/rating/rate controls before Resend call.
Normal feedback flow validates and inserts feedback before fire-and-forget email invoke.
Client feedback validator limits length/rating but is bypassable by direct function invocation.
Database feedback_reports constraints protect table insert path, not support-email direct path.
localRecommendations are bounded; body.answers is forwarded raw into Gemini prompt.
Client sends answers and localRecommendations to habit-routine function.
AI quota is per feature call; no input-size/token accounting observed.
Pro gate reviewed for AI feature access.
requireAdmin validates Supabase user email against ADMIN_EMAILS.
Privileged user actions call requireAdmin before service-role operations.
Suggested habit mutations call requireAdmin before service-role operations.
Feature flag and notification actions call requireAdmin before service-role operations.
Feedback status mutation calls requireAdmin before service-role operation.
next parameter constrained to same origin and returned as path/search/hash only.
Protected website routes and app rewrite/session behavior reviewed.
Webhook token checked and entitlement state refetched from RevenueCat before profile update.
Deletes only authenticated caller after recent sign-in check.
Authenticated function validates period/limit and uses service-only RPCs.
security invoker RPC uses auth.uid and is backed by owner FK/RLS.
Static Cloud Run app deployment and nginx headers reviewed; public unauthenticated static app exposure is intended.

evidence_note: Established product surfaces, deployment model, JWT-disabled functions, prior local audit context, and public/private config assumptions.
Reviewed service-role function gates, stored user inputs, shared secrets, HMAC tokens, RevenueCat re-fetch, and cron sinks.
Reviewed authenticated AI quota/pro-access boundaries and direct support-email provider send path.
Reviewed RLS owner policies, profile entitlement column grants, web-push endpoint schema, completion owner FK, feedback bounds, and service-only leaderboard RPC grants.
Reviewed admin email allowlist, server-action guards, service-role admin client, same-origin auth callback sanitization, and operator-controlled app rewrite destination.

Reviewed public OG rendering, client-side push subscription path, service worker token redemption, feedback validation, account deletion call path, subscription access logic, and leaderboard opt-in behavior.
Parent CSV includes generated caches, historical local worktrees, local design references, and scan artifacts. Repository deployment evidence does not make these primary product runtime surfaces.
Pattern and full-file follow-up promoted five candidates and produced suppressions recorded in finding_discovery_report.md and repository_coverage_ledger.md.

evidence: README.md documents standalone Leaderboard RPC setup after migrations; standalone get_leaderboard.sql creates SECURITY DEFINER function and only grants authenticated, but default PUBLIC execute remains and no auth.uid null fail-closed check exists. Later migrations show the missing controls.
Functions with verify_jwt=false use secondary controls before service-role impact: RevenueCat auth token, cron secret, welcome secret, or HMAC action token with expiry and field validation.

Authenticated functions verify Supabase JWTs before using user identity. Scheduled functions require shared x-cron-secret. Service-role calls are scoped to verified user IDs or scheduled batch behavior.
Admin layout and server actions check Supabase user email against ADMIN_EMAILS before service-role mutation. No unauthenticated admin action path was found in reviewed files.
Web callback sanitizes next to same-origin paths; login next path is constrained to local paths; app Supabase client disables detectSessionInUrl and uses PKCE storage handling.

Earlier owner-update profile policy is materially narrowed by 20260614120000, which revokes broad insert/update and grants only non-entitlement columns to authenticated users. Client update paths now avoid updating user_id on upsert.

OG query values render as React text inside ImageResponse and tone is table-selected; static HTML JSON-LD uses JSON.stringify for local constants; service worker notification URLs are server-generated push payload fields, not exposed as a public route sink.

Public Supabase anon, Sentry DSN, PostHog key, VAPID public key, and public URLs are client-intended. Service-role secrets are referenced only as server/Supabase secrets in reviewed deployment docs and server code.

Parent worklist includes local worktrees, generated build output, docs, tests, and creative/promo artifacts. No repository evidence showed these are deployed product/runtime surfaces; security-relevant duplicated findings were checked against the primary runtime files instead.
